message:
fix(export,html,redact): optimize text truncation, fix home-path redaction boundary, and harden HTML rendering

Text truncation optimization (export.rs):
- truncate_text() now uses a single-pass iterator (chars().by_ref()
  .take(max_len)) instead of counting all characters first and then
  taking a prefix. For long content that exceeds max_len, this avoids
  a redundant full traversal. The <=3 edge case reuses the already-
  collected preview string instead of re-iterating.

HTML renderer improvements (renderer.rs):
- render_message_group() and render_message() collapse-threshold checks
  now use an early-exit iterator that counts only up to threshold+1
  characters instead of counting all characters in potentially huge
  messages. The full count is computed only when needed for display.
- sanitize_markdown_dest_url() now short-circuits for URLs without ':'
  (the vast majority of links), avoiding a full allocation+lowercase
  for non-dangerous URLs. When a colon is present, it normalizes only
  the first 15 characters to check for javascript:/vbscript:/data:
  schemes, avoiding a full-string allocation.
- format_timestamp() now uses direct byte-range slicing instead of the
  truncate_to_char_boundary helper (which is now #[cfg(test)] only),
  since ISO 8601 timestamps are always ASCII.
- Fix SVG icon constants for ICON_SETTINGS and ICON_PENCIL which had
  malformed path data.

Home-path redaction boundary fix (redact.rs):
- Home path redaction now respects path segment boundaries. Previously,
  redacting "/home/alice" would incorrectly match "/home/alice2" because
  it used a simple string replace. The new replace_home_path_prefixes()
  function checks that the character following the home path match is
  either a path separator (/ or \) or end-of-string, preventing false
  positives on usernames that share a prefix.
- truncate_for_report() uses the same single-pass iterator optimization.
- New test: test_home_path_redaction_respects_segment_boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
